Treatment Arm(s).value: Standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: "Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone." These are the four treatment arms.

Total Participants - N.evidence: The 'Findings' section on page 1 states: 'Between Nov 27, 2013, and Dec 20, 2018, 1173 patients were enrolled (one patient subsequently withdrew consent for analysis of his data)'. The 'Results' section on page 5 also confirms '1173 patients were enrolled'. Figure 1 also shows '1173 patients enrolled and randomly assigned'.

Treatment Arm - N.value: Standard of care plus radiotherapy: n=293; Standard of care plus abiraterone: n=292; Standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone: n=291
Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Findings' section, it states: 'Between Nov 27, 2013, and Dec 20, 2018, 1173 patients were enrolled (one patient subsequently withdrew consent for analysis of his data) and assigned to receive standard of care (n=296), standard of care plus radiotherapy (n=293), standard of care plus abiraterone (n=292), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone (n=291).' The standard of care (SOC) arm (n=296) is the control arm. The other three arms are experimental arms.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'We conducted an open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2x2 factorial design (PEACE-1)'. A 2x2 factorial design inherently means there are 4 treatment arms. The text further lists these four arms: 'standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks)', 'standard of care plus radiotherapy', 'standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily)', or 'standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone'.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 3, under 'Outcomes', 'quality of life' is listed as one of the secondary endpoints. On page 9, the last paragraph states, 'The impact of toxicity on participants will be better understood after the formal analysis of quality-of-life data, at which time the triplet therapy oncological benefits might still outweigh the adverse effect of the additional toxicity.', confirming that quality of life data was collected and will be analyzed.

Control Arm.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone..., or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.' The 'standard of care' arm is the comparison group that does not receive the experimental treatments (abiraterone or radiotherapy). The 'Findings' section on page 1 also mentions patients 'assigned to receive standard of care (n=296)'.

NCT.evidence: Found in the "Methods" section on page 1, explicitly stating the trial is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov, NCT01957436.

PubMed ID.evidence: The document provides a DOI (https://doi.org/10.1016/S0140-6736(22)00367-1) but no explicit PubMed ID.

Trial Name.evidence: Found in the title of the article on page 1, where the trial is identified as "(PEACE-1)".

Author.evidence: The first author listed on page 1 is Karim Fizazi, followed by many others, making "et al." appropriate.

Year.evidence: Found on page 1 under "Published Online", indicating the publication date as April 8, 2022.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is presented as an "Article" with extensive content including detailed methods, findings, and discussion, spanning multiple pages, which is characteristic of a full publication rather than an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Found in the title and the "Methods" section on page 1, explicitly stating "phase 3 study".

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The article describes the design and findings of the "PEACE-1" trial as an "open-label, randomised, phase 3 study" and presents its primary results, indicating it is an original report of the trial's findings.

Control Arm - N.value: 296 (Standard of care); 293 (Standard of care plus radiotherapy)
Control Arm - N.evidence: Found in the 'Findings' section on page 1, which states: '...assigned to receive standard of care (n=296), standard of care plus radiotherapy (n=293)...'. These two groups serve as the control arms in the 2x2 factorial design, not receiving the investigational abiraterone.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document mentions 'quality of life' as a secondary endpoint (page 3, 'Outcomes' section) and states that 'The impact of toxicity on participants will be better understood after the formal analysis of quality-of-life data' (page 9). However, it does not specify the name of the quality of life scale used, nor does it provide any extracted values or results from such a scale.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 5, in the 'Results' section, it states: 'For the overall survival analyses, the median follow-up periods from randomisation to the cutoff date of June 1, 2021, were 4.41 years for the overall population'. This value was converted from years to months (4.41 * 12 = 52.92).

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study, 'PEACE-1', is explicitly stated in the title of the article: 'Abiraterone plus prednisone added to androgen deprivation therapy and docetaxel in de novo metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (PEACE-1): a multicentre, open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2 x 2 factorial design'. It is also mentioned multiple times throughout the document, for example, in the 'Methods' section on page 1: 'We conducted an open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2x2 factorial design (PEACE-1) at 77 hospitals across Belgium, France, Ireland, Italy, Romania, Spain, and Switzerland.' The study is also registered with ClinicalTrials.gov, NCT01957436.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The study focuses on 'de novo metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer' (title, summary, introduction), which refers to synchronous metastases. The 'Randomisation and masking' section on page 3 states that patients were stratified by 'disease extent or burden based on metastatic status (lymph node metastases only vs bone metastases [with or without lymph node metastases] vs visceral metastases)' and 'high-volume versus low-volume metastatic burden classification'. Table 1 and Figure 3 on pages 6 and 10, respectively, present results by 'Metastatic burdens' (High/Low burden), which are prognostic groups within the synchronous population.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The 'Discussion' section on page 11 explicitly states: 'Nonetheless, as PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC, it is still unclear whether this triple therapy might benefit patients with metachronous mCSPC. Our findings cannot directly address whether this triplet systemic combination is superior to ADT and abiraterone.' This indicates that the study did not include or report on metachronous prognostic groups.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The 'Randomisation and masking' section on page 3 mentions 'patient stratification according to high-volume versus low-volume metastatic burden classification (as defined in the CHAARTED study² [high volume was defined as the presence of visceral metastases or at least four bone lesions with one beyond the vertebral bodies and pelvis])'. Table 1 on page 6 includes a 'Metastatic burdens' section with 'High burden' reported. The 'Results' section on page 7 and Figure 2F on page 8, as well as Table 2 on page 9, present outcomes specifically for patients with 'high-volume metastatic burden'.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The 'Randomisation and masking' section on page 3 mentions 'patient stratification according to high-volume versus low-volume metastatic burden classification'. Table 1 on page 6 includes a 'Metastatic burdens' section with 'Low burden' reported. The 'Results' section on page 7 and Figure 2E on page 8, as well as Table 2 on page 9, present outcomes specifically for patients with 'low metastatic burden'.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that participants were randomly assigned to 'standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks)' as one of the treatment arms. Androgen deprivation therapy is a class of hormonal therapy, and docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma', which means synchronous metastases. The 'Discussion' section on page 11 confirms that 'PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC'. In the 'ADT with docetaxel population' (the population of interest for abiraterone efficacy), the 'SOC plus abiraterone groups' (treatment arm) had n=355 participants (Table 1, page 6). Therefore, 355 (100%) of participants in the treatment arm had synchronous metastases.

The 'Methods' section on page 2 states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma', which means synchronous metastases. The 'Discussion' section on page 11 confirms that 'PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC'. In the 'ADT with docetaxel population' (the population of interest for abiraterone efficacy), the 'SOC without abiraterone groups' (control arm) had n=355 participants (Table 1, page 6). Therefore, 355 (100%) of participants in the control arm had synchronous metastases.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma'. The 'Discussion' section on page 11 explicitly states: 'Nonetheless, as PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC, it is still unclear whether this triple therapy might benefit patients with metachronous mCSPC.' This confirms that no patients in the study, including the treatment arm, had metachronous metastases.

The 'Methods' section on page 2 states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma'. The 'Discussion' section on page 11 explicitly states: 'Nonetheless, as PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC, it is still unclear whether this triple therapy might benefit patients with metachronous mCSPC.' This confirms that no patients in the study, including the control arm, had metachronous metastases.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: radiographic progression-free survival and overall survival
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'Methods' section, it states: 'The coprimary endpoints were radiographic progression-free survival and overall survival.'

Region - N (%) | Europe | Treatment.evidence: Derived by summing the N (%) for all listed countries (Belgium: 29 (5%), France: 458 (79%), Ireland: 30 (5%), Italy: 1 (<1%), Romania: 4 (1%), Spain: 55 (9%), Switzerland: 6 (1%)) under 'Country' in 'Table 1: Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population' for the 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=583)' column. The sum of N is 29+458+30+1+4+55+6 = 583. The sum of percentages is approximately 100%.

Region - N (%) | Europe | Control.evidence: Derived by summing the N (%) for all listed countries (Belgium: 25 (4%), France: 462 (78%), Ireland: 30 (5%), Italy: 3 (1%), Romania: 5 (1%), Spain: 56 (10%), Switzerland: 8 (1%)) under 'Country' in 'Table 1: Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population' for the 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=589)' column. The sum of N is 25+462+30+3+5+56+8 = 589. The sum of percentages is approximately 100%.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC)-free survival, serious-genito-urinary-event-free survival, prostate-cancer-specificsurvival, time to next skeletal-related event, PSA response rate, prognostic study of serum PSA measured 6-8 months after initiation of systemic therapy, time to pain pro-gression, time to chemotherapy for CRPC, quality of life, changes in bone mineral density, correlation of biomarkers with outcome, event rate per 100 person-years of treatment analysis, and toxicity.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 3, under the 'Outcomes' section, the text explicitly states: 'The secondary endpoints were castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC)-free survival, serious-genito-urinary-event-free survival, prostate-cancer-specificsurvival, time to next skeletal-related event, PSA response rate, prognostic study of serum PSA measured 6-8 months after initiation of systemic therapy, time to pain pro-gression, time to chemotherapy for CRPC, quality of life, changes in bone mineral density, correlation of biomarkers with outcome, event rate per 100 person-years of treatment analysis, and toxicity.'

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: Derived from Figure 1 'Trial profile' on page 5. The 'Treatment' arm is defined as the 'SOC plus abiraterone groups', which includes '292 assigned to SOC plus abiraterone' and '291 assigned to SOC plus abiraterone plus radiotherapy', totaling 583 patients. Within these groups, 177 and 178 patients, respectively, received docetaxel, totaling 355 patients. The percentage is (355 / 583) * 100 = 60.89%, rounded to 60.9%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: Derived from Figure 1 'Trial profile' on page 5. The 'Control' arm is defined as the 'SOC without abiraterone groups', which includes '296 assigned to SOC' and '293 assigned to SOC plus radiotherapy', totaling 589 patients. Within these groups, 178 and 177 patients, respectively, received docetaxel, totaling 355 patients. The percentage is (355 / 589) * 100 = 60.27%, rounded to 60.3%.

It does not report Overall Response Rate (ORR) or its components (CR, PR, SD, PD) in terms of N (%) for the treatment arm.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks) plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that participants were randomly assigned to four arms, including 'standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily)'. The 'standard of care' itself is described as 'androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks'. This arm represents a primary experimental intervention involving abiraterone, which is highlighted in the paper's title.

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: The document does not explicitly report 'Time To Progression of Serum Androgen (TTPSA)' as a distinct endpoint with specific values for the treatment arm. While 'CRPC-free survival' is reported and includes a 'confirmed PSA rise' as part of its definition, it is a composite endpoint that also includes radiographic progression or death, and therefore not solely TTPSA.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: The document does not explicitly report 'Time To Progression of Serum Androgen (TTPSA)' as a distinct endpoint with specific values for the control arm. While 'CRPC-free survival' is reported and includes a 'confirmed PSA rise' as part of its definition, it is a composite endpoint that also includes radiographic progression or death, and therefore not solely TTPSA.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document describes the study design as an "open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2x2 factorial design" (page 1, Methods) and acknowledges "limitations inherently associated with open-label trials" (page 9, Discussion). While 'open-label' implies a lack of blinding, which is a criterion of evidence that can introduce bias, the document does not provide an explicit overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT).

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document states on page 7: "Overall, the median abiraterone treatment duration before discontinuation was 33.2 months (95% CI 25.5-43-2) in the ADT without docetaxel population and 34.1 months (30.0-43.5) in the ADT with docetaxel population." These values represent the median abiraterone treatment duration for subgroups of the treatment arm (those receiving abiraterone with or without docetaxel), but a single median duration for the entire treatment arm (all patients receiving abiraterone) is not reported.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The control arm is defined as "SOC without abiraterone groups" (page 6, Table 1). Since participants in the control arm did not receive abiraterone, the median duration of abiraterone treatment for this arm is 0 months. The question asks for the median duration of 'on-treatment therapy', and the relevant 'treatment' being evaluated for duration in the text is abiraterone.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: On page 6, in the 'ADT with docetaxel population (n=710)*' section, it is stated: 'compared with SOC (including docetaxel) without abiraterone, the addition of abiraterone reduced the number of deaths from 151 to 121'. The value '121' corresponds to the number of deaths in the abiraterone (treatment) group.

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: On page 6, in the 'ADT with docetaxel population (n=710)*' section, it is stated: 'compared with SOC (including docetaxel) without abiraterone, the addition of abiraterone reduced the number of deaths from 151 to 121'. The value '151' corresponds to the number of deaths in the without abiraterone (control) group.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The 'Summary' section states: 'We aimed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of abiraterone plus prednisone, with or without radiotherapy, in addition to standard of care.' The 'Methods' section further details the randomisation groups, including 'standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.' This indicates that abiraterone plus prednisone and radiotherapy are the additional therapies.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: On page 7, in the paragraph 'In the ADT with docetaxel safety population', it states '217 (63%) of 347 patients who received abiraterone... had at least one severe adverse event (grade 3 or worse)'. This is confirmed in Table 3 on page 11, row 'Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events', column 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=347)'.

On page 7, in the paragraph 'In the ADT with docetaxel safety population', it states '...versus 181 (52%) of 350 who did not receive abiraterone had at least one severe adverse event (grade 3 or worse)'. This is confirmed in Table 3 on page 11, row 'Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events', column 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=350)'.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document reports 'Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events' as all-cause in Table 3 and related text. It does not provide a specific N (%) for 'treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher' adverse events as a general category.

The document reports 'Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events' as all-cause in Table 3 and related text. It does not provide a specific N (%) for 'treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher' adverse events as a general category.
The document reports 'Fatal (grade 5) adverse events' in Table 3 on page 11 and in the text on page 7. However, it does not explicitly state that these fatal events were 'treatment-related'.
The document reports 'Fatal (grade 5) adverse events' in Table 3 on page 11 and in the text on page 7. However, it does not explicitly state that these fatal events were 'treatment-related'.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population', under 'Primary outcomes in the overall population', row 'Radiographic progression-free survival', for 'SOC with abiraterone groups'. The median PFS is 4.5 years. The Hazard Ratio of 0.54 (99.9% CI 0.41-0.71) is for abiraterone vs no abiraterone in the overall population, as also stated in the 'Findings' section on page 1.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population', under 'Primary outcomes in the overall population', row 'Radiographic progression-free survival', for 'SOC without abiraterone groups'. The median PFS is 2.2 years.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population', under 'Primary outcomes in the overall population', 'Radiographic progression-free survival', 'SOC with abiraterone groups', Median (years) column, value is 4.5. Converted to months: 4.5 * 12 = 54 months.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population', under 'Primary outcomes in the overall population', 'Radiographic progression-free survival', 'SOC without abiraterone groups', Median (years) column, value is 2.2. Converted to months: 2.2 * 12 = 26.4 months.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median overall survival in years, not a specific OS rate (percentage). For the overall population, the median overall survival for SOC plus abiraterone groups (treatment) was 5.7 years (Table 2, page 9).

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The document reports median overall survival in years, not a specific OS rate (percentage). For the overall population, the median overall survival for SOC without abiraterone groups (control) was 4.7 years (Table 2, page 9).

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Treatment.evidence: The study included patients with 'de novo metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer' (synchronous metastasis), as stated in the 'Interpretation' section on page 11. However, specific OS rates (percentages) for synchronous metastasis in the treatment arm are not reported separately from the overall population or other subgroups. Median overall survival in years is reported for the overall population and by metastatic burden, but not specifically for synchronous metastasis as a distinct category with a percentage rate.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Treatment.evidence: The 'Interpretation' section on page 11 explicitly states: 'Nonetheless, as PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC, it is still unclear whether this triple therapy might benefit patients with metachronous mCSPC. Our findings cannot directly address whether this triplet systemic combination is superior to ADT and abiraterone.' Therefore, OS rates for metachronous metastasis in the treatment arm are not reported.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median overall survival in years, not a specific OS rate (percentage). For patients with high-volume metastatic burden in the ADT with docetaxel population, the median overall survival for SOC plus abiraterone groups (treatment) was 5.1 years (Table 2, page 9) or 5.14 years ('Results' section, page 7).

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Control.evidence: The document reports median overall survival in years, not a specific OS rate (percentage). For patients with high-volume metastatic burden in the ADT with docetaxel population, the median overall survival for SOC without abiraterone groups (control) was 3.5 years (Table 2, page 9) or 3.47 years ('Results' section, page 7).

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median overall survival in years, not a specific OS rate (percentage). For patients with low-volume metastatic burden in the ADT with docetaxel population, the median overall survival for SOC plus abiraterone groups (treatment) was 'NR' (Not reached) (Table 2, page 9). The 'Results' section on page 7 also states that 'no conclusive response in overall survival could be shown for patients with low metastatic burden' due to immature data.
